title: When Evaluating External Ideas: - Ask:
subtitle: Embrace Outsider Ideas
description:
When evaluating external ideas:
- Ask: "Is this idea or product effective, regardless of where it came from?"
- Compare: Assess its potential objectively against internal solutions.
- Collaborate: Consider how external knowledge might complement your existing methods.

Example: Your team resists using an external tool because it’s not developed in-house. Challenge this by focusing on its benefits and efficiency.

lifeOsSource.checkIn: Check in when you adopt or explore an external idea that adds value.

faq.question: How do I track my progress with this habit?
faq.answer: Use the check-in method described above and track it in the Life OS app for consistent progress monitoring.
faq.answerHtml: Use the check-in method described above and track it in the Life OS app for consistent progress monitoring.

faq.question: What if I miss a day or forget to do this habit?
faq.answer: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.
faq.answerHtml: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.

faq.question: How long does it take to form this habit?
faq.answer: Research shows habits can take 21-66 days to form. Focus on consistency rather than perfection, and use the app to build momentum.

howTo.title: Define the metric baseline
howTo.summary: Choose a primary outcome to measure, such as time saved or error count. Clearly state the baseline value to establish a reference point for your experiment.

howTo.title: Design a 3-day micro-test
howTo.summary: Create a small test that runs over three days. Define the scope, procedure, and acceptance threshold to ensure clarity and focus during the evaluation.

howTo.title: Run the 3-day experiment
howTo.summary: Conduct the test in the actual context where the tool will be used. Log the results immediately after each task to maintain accuracy and minimize bias.

howTo.title: Evaluate and document findings
howTo.summary: Analyze the results based on the defined metrics. Document the decision to adopt, adapt, or archive the idea, including the rationale behind it.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results and insights in the Brali LifeOS app to track progress and facilitate future evaluations. Access it at https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/evaluate-external-ideas-objectively/.
